task_completion.description: Create at least three defined rooms for the Waiting Area, Service Counters, and Back Office.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D plan view in Sweet Home 3D for at least three distinct room areas. These should be demarcated either by text labels ('Waiting Area', 'Service Counters', 'Back Office') or by distinct floor colors.

task_completion.description: Construct at least two interior partition walls to separate the public-facing area from the employee-only back office.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the 2D plan or 3D view for at least two newly drawn interior walls inside the building shell that separate the space into distinct sections.

task_completion.description: Install at least two doors for secure access between zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for at least two door icons in the 2D plan or 3D door models placed within the interior partition walls.

task_completion.description: Furnish the Waiting Area with at least 30 chairs or benches.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Count or estimate the seating in the designated Waiting Area. There should be a large cluster of at least 30 chairs or benches visible in the 2D plan or 3D view.

task_completion.description: Construct the Service Counters using at least eight desks and eight office chairs.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the Service Counters zone for an arrangement of at least 8 desks and 8 corresponding office chairs.

task_completion.description: Equip the Back Office with at least four administrative storage units.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the Back Office zone for at least 4 storage units, such as shelving units, bookcases, or filing cabinets.
Look for the agent clicking the 'Save' icon, interacting with a save dialog, or the title bar showing the filename without an unsaved changes indicator (like an asterisk).

integrity.description: The agent used the Sweet Home 3D graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Sweet Home 3D application window is visible and actively interacted with (e.g., dragging and dropping furniture, drawing walls), rather than the agent using terminal commands to modify the file directly.

integrity.description: The agent built the layout from the empty starter shell.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show a progression from an empty building shell to a fully furnished layout, proving the agent did not simply load a pre-completed file.

integrity.description: The 3D view pane accurately reflects the 2D floor plan additions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The bottom 3D view pane in Sweet Home 3D should show the 3D models of the walls, doors, and furniture corresponding to the items placed in the top 2D grid.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task uses a starter file named 'dmv_starter.sh3d', which is an empty building shell. The agent must transform this shell into the DMV layout using Sweet Home 3D. The total possible score is 100 points, with a passing threshold of 70 points.
